office_does: The sheriff leads county law enforcement, jail and court-security functions, emergency response, staffing, and the office budget.
race_blurb: Three law-enforcement professionals seek the open sheriff position: Kohl St. Peter, Undersheriff Monty Huber, and Connell Police Chief Chris Lee.

candidates.summary: Lee is Connell's police chief and a veteran officer focused on deputy support, victims, dangerous offenders, partnerships, and fiscal discipline.
highlights: Has more than 25 years in regional law enforcement and serves as police chief [S1]
Supports incarceration of dangerous offenders, victims, transparency, and fiscal discipline [S1]

safety.basis: He explicitly emphasizes law enforcement and incarceration of dangerous offenders.

experience.basis: More than 25 years in policing and current police-chief service provide executive law-enforcement experience.

candidates.summary: St. Peter is a career officer focused on integrity, officer wellness, technology, and long-range organizational planning.
highlights: Has served in law enforcement since 2012 [S1]
Emphasizes command integrity, officer wellness, modernization, and planning [S1]

experience.basis: He has substantial policing experience but no sheriff-command or elected record.

candidates.summary: Huber is the county undersheriff, Army veteran, and former councilmember focused on training, accreditation, deputy support, trust, and behavioral-health response.
highlights: Has more than 23 years in the sheriff's office and serves as undersheriff [S1]
Supports training, accreditation, community trust, and responses to mental health and addiction [S1]

safety.basis: His explicit attention to mental-health and addiction responses gives a services direction, balanced by a law-enforcement command role.

experience.basis: Sheriff-command, operational, military, and municipal elected experience provide an extensive record.
